title: hieracium murorum subsp. sylvaticum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. sylvaticum, also known as the woodland hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a mild sedative. Hieracium murorum subsp. sylvaticum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use.
